abstract: Returns the cost to travel from this node to the specified, directly connected, node.
overview:
## Return Value

The real cost of travel along the specified connection; higher values indicate higher cost.

## Discussion

The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph] class uses this method in pathfinding between nodes—the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method finds the lowest-cost path between a specified pair of nodes. For the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraphNode] base class, the cost of travel along any connection is 1, so the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method simply counts connections, finding the path that involves traveling through the fewest number of nodes. Because the subclasses [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraphNode2D] and [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGridGraphNode] add geometry information to each node, those classes’ implementation of this method calculates the distance between nodes in 2D space, and the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method finds the path with the minimum total distance traveled.

Subclasses can implement this method to add other information to the cost. For example, a game might use higher costs to represent travel through regions of a map that are difficult for a character to traverse.

# cost(to:)

**Instance Method**

Returns the cost to travel from this node to the specified, directly connected, node.

## Declaration

```swift
func cost(to node: GKGraphNode) -> Float
```

## Parameters

- **node**: A node from this node’s [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraphNode/connectedNodes] list.

## Return Value

The real cost of travel along the specified connection; higher values indicate higher cost.

## Discussion

The [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph] class uses this method in pathfinding between nodes—the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method finds the lowest-cost path between a specified pair of nodes. For the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraphNode] base class, the cost of travel along any connection is 1, so the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method simply counts connections, finding the path that involves traveling through the fewest number of nodes. Because the subclasses [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraphNode2D] and [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGridGraphNode] add geometry information to each node, those classes’ implementation of this method calculates the distance between nodes in 2D space, and the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKGraph/findPath(from:to:)] method finds the path with the minimum total distance traveled.

Subclasses can implement this method to add other information to the cost. For example, a game might use higher costs to represent travel through regions of a map that are difficult for a character to traverse.

## Computing Traversal Costs

- **estimatedCost(to:)**: Returns an underestimate of the cost of travel from this node to the specified node.
